We are an elite software development company, experts in Python / Django and .NET solutions, trusted by global enterprises and skilled in industry-specific software solutions.
On-premise and cloud solutions tailor-made for real estate evaluation and management.
Websites that highlight corporate values and pharmaceutical products. Internal systems and CRMs.
Fleet showcase, brand communication and exhibition management for global maritime transport companies.
Development, design, analysis and consulting for international fintech corporations.
Immersive, pixel-perfect websites to showcase artwork and promote cultural actions.
Tailored solutions for the banking & insurance sector, with emphasis on security. Website development, e-banking application processing, e-payment platforms and more.

original text:
IT IS TRAGIC TO SEE INTELLIGENT BUSINESS EXPERTS THROW ALL CAUTION TO THE WIND AND BLOW THEIR BRAINS OUT WHEN DEVELOPING A RESORT.
Acting as we do as the first line of defence in a developer's quest for financing, we see the best and worst of the World's Developers and their very obvious blinders and blunders.
There are a couple of wonderful TV shows on the television in the Americas and Europe that deal with entrepreneurs pitching their projects to a panel of experts. Questions are asked and the entrepreneurs have but a few minutes to gain the interest from a panellist and obtain the investment directly.
We do similar work on behalf of an Investment Bank but we focus mainly on hotels & resorts.
Here is a cynical look at the hard work of many an entrepreneur and where many of them seem to go askew.
Great Idea! Let's just do it! We have decided we have this great idea and we need your money to back our risk, no questions asked. Appealing to my sympathetic ear because I know resorts, the developer fails to realize that the investors & lenders want independent advice and proof from experts that the project will work.
Find me a partner, quick. Isn't it funny how developers look for partners when they really need a lender or they are looking for a lender when they really need a partner. Yes the Capital Stack is part of the financial architecture and it takes as much expertise to structure the Capital Stack as it does the Chimney Stack. Some owners & developers fail to realize that they don't need to give up valuable equity to get the financing. On many occasions we can structure the loans to preserve the equity.
Yup, we'll create our own brand. No need to pay those Big-Boys a ton of fees when all we need to do is create our own. We have this idea for a Spa and we have already designed the name and logos. The reality is that your brand will need to have name recognition in the geographical markets that feed your property..........plus 14 other critical components.
Hurry, the World Cup is coming in 2 years. The Prime Minister has asked us to hurry our plans along and build the hotel for the World Cup. It is coming in two years........for two weeks! Hotels & resorts have been built for the Olympics, a Convention, the Pan Am & Pan Asian games and countless other lousy excuses. They need to be planned for 2 decades of success, not 2 weeks, but why would the Prime Minister care?
Just sell it. A property ready for development has the plans bought and paid for, approvals in place, an operator ready to take it on and everything ready but he tells his Broker, 'sell it', not realizing that if all is ready, develop it and then sell it for a multiple of what he would sell it for now.... but why would the Broker care?
Need grocery money too! Know how to make a small fortune in the resort industry?Start with a large fortune!! Yes, many spend their whole fortune on architects, plans, approvals and glitz and then start looking for the financing but are so desperate that they need not only the $75 million but some extra for the groceries but why would the architect care?
I want it now, not in 3 months. Faced with the reality that it takes 3-4 months to source the financing and having spent three years already looking, there is no patience to take the 3-4 months necessary and do it properly.
My cousin did the Study & it works. So many developers do the projections and financial assumptions through close friends or family or even the village accountant not realizing that the capital sources will want to have a recent study done by an independent, recognized Consultant company and that the value of one done by someone close to the developer has zero credibility but why would the village accountant care.
What's wrong with 50 rooms & 2 restaurants? Do we really have to explain!!
Where's the budget? No sir, a Construction budget is not a development budget!! So many developers prepare a detailed construction and fit-out budget not realizing that a full development budget contains many other line items which will have to be covered. For us, this is a clear sign that we are dealing with students.
I make my wife furious when I go to the Doctor and offer my own diagnosis!! I think this situation is somewhat similar. Allow the specialists the freedom of telling you if your property will live or die and listen very, very carefully. The fortune you lose may be your own.
My ideas, your money and your risk.
Creating a brand is simple stuff.
Sell low so others can benefit.
Spent it all. Nothing left to finish.
Patience is not needed for financing.
For any and all enquiries on financing of hotels and resorts, consult a specialist and make sure that you have all the financing before spending on anything.

original text:
10mm pistol (Fallout Shelter) - The Vault Fallout Wiki - Everything you need to know about Fallout 76, Fallout 4, New Vegas and more!
For an overview of 10mm pistol models throughout the Fallout series, see 10mm pistol.
10mm pistol is a weapon in Fallout Shelter. There are six variations of the weapon, including a unique legendary 10mm pistol.
The following section is transcluded from 10mm pistol. To modify, please edit the source page.
10mm pistols are sidearms using the 10mm ammunition that was common before the Great War. They were used extensively by the Bureau of Alcohol, Drugs, Tobacco, Firearms and Lasers, while in the military such pistols were being phased out in favor of energy weapons. Their ruggedness, reliability, and firepower made them a popular choice among wastelanders and thus a staple of wasteland firefights.
The large-framed, gas-operated N99 was selected as the standard issue conventional sidearm of the United States military, following the phaseout of the N80 in 2051. The long-barreled pistol remained in service for 26 years, until the Great War destroyed the United States. The pistol is designed to withstand the most extreme conditions, which was one of the requirements for a new service sidearm. It can also be repaired easily by anyone with even the most basic technical knowledge. This includes both clearing misfeeds and restoring a unit to working condition after long exposure to elements without maintenance.
These features also make it popular with wastelanders, who appreciate the ease of maintenance. It remains a common weapon, as even though many civilian units were ruined, they can easily be restored by anyone with some repair knowledge. Even damaged units can be used reliably, though they will offer subpar performance and jam frequently. The number of manufactured pistols means that parts are easy to come by, however.
Common 2 Not the most reliable pistol in the world, but it'll do in a pinch.
Common 2-3 The most common handgun found in the wasteland.
Common 2-4 Higher damage capacity, freshly oiled. Oh, yeah.
Rare 2-5 Lovingly engineered and modified. A mercenary's dream.
Rare 2-6 Bring justice to the wasteland, one Raider corpse at a time.
Legendary 2-7 One hero. One handgun. And thus, a legend was born.
↑ Fallout 4 loading screen hints: "Small, dependable, reasonably powerful and widely available, the 10mm Pistol has been a staple of Wasteland combat since the bombs first fell."
↑ The Lone Wanderer: "Your gun is jammed? Can I take a look at it?"
Pek: "Sure, I guess. Can you fix it?"
The Lone Wanderer: "Sure can. Here, just pull this back to clear the chamber."
Pek: "Wow... that's... that's easy. Now at least I can shoot back at those mutant bastards. Are you ready?"
↑ 4.0 4.1 Citadel terminal entries: N99 10mm Pistol: "The 10mm N99 sidearm was standard issue military gear following the phase out of the N80 in 2051. The N99 was known for its ability to survive in the harshest of conditions and in many tests, the weapon was able to reliably be restored to working condition after an extended period of time exposed to hard elements and without maintenance.
The N99 proved to stand up to its claims and as a result, is one of the most common weapons found in use in the Wasteland. While many civilian weapons were ruined beyond use in the exterior conditions following the war, the N99 is commonly able to be easily restored to working order by anyone with the most basic knowledge of firearm mechanics.
However, an N99 in poor condition can often deliver low target and armor penetration, frequent jamming, and greatly reduced accuracy. Fortunately, the weapon was produced in great numbers and units are available for replacement parts."
This page was last edited on 20 July 2015, at 07:27.

original text:
Just and unjust step we onwards, wise men.
Umbra, sure we're ever fallen again.
Each footprint, our ellipsis dogged by fear.
Choose your course against the winds of terror.
Hold steady in the killing cold of sin.
Impenetrable shade of our error.
The color of each soul He bought that night.

original text:
Thonglor being Sukhumvit Soi 55 is the playground and meeting spot for Bangkok's young, urban and hip types. The area is well known for having some of the best Thai nightclubs, boutique malls as well as trendy restaurants and upmarket bars. Thong Lo wakes up when the sun sets. The Thai high-so crowd descends on Thonglor for a fun and intoxicating night out.
If you want the experience the nightlife of middle class Bangkok then this area has more upmarket clubs and pubs than where the tourists go. Thong Lo wakes up at 10 at night!
City Lodge Sukhumvit 19 Hotel - good reputation and well located in Bangkok.
41 Suite Bangkok Hotel - very good hotel even on a budget while in Thailand.
Adelphi Suites Hotel - excellent hotel with a great location in Bangkok. A hotel you will not regret.
Somerset Sukhumvit Thonglor - Well known hotel with an excellent reputation.
Centre Point Sukhumvit - very good hotel with great staff and location all within budget.
Wellness Residence - good hotel if you are looking for something in a lower price.
Rainforest Guest House - great for backpackers while in Thailand. good location and price.
Sawasdee Sukhumvit Inn Hotel - Well known budget hotel chain in Thailand.
Meevasana Wine & Art Gallery - if you are into art and good wine then the best imported wines.
Ardel's Third Place Gallery - looking for contemporary art in Thailand then this will satisfy you.
Blu-O (Bowling) - bowling alleys are your thing then this will not get any better than this.
Bangkok Planetarium - for the person who loves Space and looking at the galaxy while in Thailand.
Arena 10 - not well know to foreigners but a good place to spend your time watching local football.
K Village Mall - wide selection of restaurants and stores at the mall.
J Avenue - outdoor living at its best with a great mixtures of restaurants and stores.
Nara - a reasonable price tasty Thai meal while on the go.
The Iron Fairies - an excellent Jazz bar and restaurant where magic happens.
Octave Rooftop Bar - One of the best rooftop bars in Bangkok.
Demo  -  If you are looking for Electro, Hiphop and band music then this is it.
Muse  -  Food on the roof is excellent with great music.
Nunglen & Escobar  -  One of the best nightclubs in Thong Lor.

original text:
APOB has 20,747 functional associations with biological entities spanning 8 categories (molecular profile, organism, disease, phenotype or trait, chemical, functional term, phrase or reference, structural feature, cell line, cell type or tissue, gene, protein or microRNA) extracted from 80 datasets.
Click the + buttons to view associations for APOB from the datasets below.
Allen Brain Atlas Adult Mouse Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles tissues with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other tissues from the Allen Brain Atlas Adult Mouse Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
Allen Brain Atlas Developing Human Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles by Microarray tissue samples with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other tissue samples from the Allen Brain Atlas Developing Human Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles by Microarray dataset.
Allen Brain Atlas Prenatal Human Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles tissues with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other tissues from the Allen Brain Atlas Prenatal Human Brain Tissue Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
BioGPS Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles cell lines with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other cell lines from the BioGPS Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
BioGPS Human Cell Type and Tissue Gene Expression Profiles cell types and tissues with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other cell types and tissues from the BioGPS Human Cell Type and Tissue Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
BioGPS Mouse Cell Type and Tissue Gene Expression Profiles cell types and tissues with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other cell types and tissues from the BioGPS Mouse Cell Type and Tissue Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
CCLE Cell Line Gene CNV Profiles cell lines with high or low copy number of APOB gene relative to other cell lines from the CCLE Cell Line Gene CNV Profiles dataset.
CCLE Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles cell lines with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other cell lines from the CCLE Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
CHEA Transcription Factor Binding Site Profiles transcription factor binding site profiles with transcription factor binding evidence at the promoter of APOB gene from the CHEA Transcription Factor Binding Site Profiles dataset.
CHEA Transcription Factor Targets transcription factors binding the promoter of APOB gene in low- or high-throughput transcription factor functional studies from the CHEA Transcription Factor Targets dataset.
ClinVar Gene-Phenotype Associations phenotypes associated with APOB gene from the curated ClinVar Gene-Phenotype Associations dataset.
CMAP Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Small Molecules small molecule perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the CMAP Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Small Molecules dataset.
COMPARTMENTS Curated Protein Localization Evidence Scores cellular components containing APOB protein from the COMPARTMENTS Curated Protein Localization Evidence Scores dataset.
COMPARTMENTS Experimental Protein Localization Evidence Scores cellular components containing APOB protein in low- or high-throughput protein localization assays from the COMPARTMENTS Experimental Protein Localization Evidence Scores dataset.
COMPARTMENTS Text-mining Protein Localization Evidence Scores cellular components co-occuring with APOB protein in abstracts of biomedical publications from the COMPARTMENTS Text-mining Protein Localization Evidence Scores dataset.
COSMIC Cell Line Gene CNV Profiles cell lines with high or low copy number of APOB gene relative to other cell lines from the COSMIC Cell Line Gene CNV Profiles dataset.
COSMIC Cell Line Gene Mutation Profiles cell lines with APOB gene mutations from the COSMIC Cell Line Gene Mutation Profiles dataset.
CTD Gene-Chemical Interactions chemicals interacting with APOB gene/protein from the curated CTD Gene-Chemical Interactions dataset.
CTD Gene-Disease Associations diseases associated with APOB gene/protein from the curated CTD Gene-Disease Associations dataset.
dbGAP Gene-Trait Associations traits associated with APOB gene in GWAS and other genetic association datasets from the dbGAP Gene-Trait Associations dataset.
DISEASES Curated Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores diseases involving APOB gene from the DISEASES Curated Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores dataset.
DISEASES Experimental Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores diseases associated with APOB gene in GWAS datasets from the DISEASES Experimental Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores dataset.
DISEASES Text-mining Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores diseases co-occuring with APOB gene in abstracts of biomedical publications from the DISEASES Text-mining Gene-Disease Assocation Evidence Scores dataset.
ENCODE Histone Modification Site Profiles histone modification site profiles with high histone modification abundance at APOB gene from the ENCODE Histone Modification Site Profiles dataset.
ENCODE Transcription Factor Binding Site Profiles transcription factor binding site profiles with transcription factor binding evidence at the promoter of APOB gene from the ENCODE Transcription Factor Binding Site Profiles dataset.
ENCODE Transcription Factor Targets transcription factors binding the promoter of APOB gene in ChIP-seq datasets from the ENCODE Transcription Factor Targets dataset.
ESCAPE Omics Signatures of Genes and Proteins for Stem Cells PubMedIDs of publications reporting gene signatures containing APOB from the ESCAPE Omics Signatures of Genes and Proteins for Stem Cells dataset.
GAD Gene-Disease Associations diseases associated with APOB gene in GWAS and other genetic association datasets from the GAD Gene-Disease Associations dataset.
GAD High Level Gene-Disease Associations diseases associated with APOB gene in GWAS and other genetic association datasets from the GAD High Level Gene-Disease Associations dataset.
GDSC Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles cell lines with high or low expression of APOB gene relative to other cell lines from the GDSC Cell Line Gene Expression Profiles dataset.
GeneRIF Biological Term Annotations biological terms co-occuring with APOB gene in literature-supported statements describing functions of genes from the GeneRIF Biological Term Annotations dataset.
GeneSigDB Published Gene Signatures PubMedIDs of publications reporting gene signatures containing APOB from the GeneSigDB Published Gene Signatures dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Diseases disease perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Diseases dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Gene Perturbations gene perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Gene Perturbations dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Kinase Perturbations kinase perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Kinase Perturbations dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Small Molecules small molecule perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Small Molecules dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Transcription Factor Perturbations transcription factor perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Transcription Factor Perturbations dataset.
GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Viral Infections virus perturbations changing expression of APOB gene from the GEO Signatures of Differentially Expressed Genes for Viral Infections dataset.
GO Biological Process Annotations biological processes involving APOB gene from the curated GO Biological Process Annotations dataset.
GO Cellular Component Annotations cellular components containing APOB protein from the curated GO Cellular Component Annotations dataset.
GO Molecular Function Annotations molecular functions performed by APOB gene from the curated GO Molecular Function Annotations dataset.

processed_text:
APOB has 20,747 functional associations with biological entities spanning 8 categories (molecular profile, organism, disease, phenotype or trait, chemical, functional term, phrase or reference, structural feature, cell line, cell type or tissue, gene, protein or microRNA) extracted from 80 datasets.
Click the + buttons to view associations for APOB from the datasets below.
